P1.name: Tel Aviv AI & Deep Tech

stacks.description: Coordinating behaviors (S2: extensive network infrastructure) co-occur with mediating behaviors (S5: intermediary structures). X-side of S2 (university support units, partnerships, investment networks) plausibly sustained by X-side of S5 (accelerators, platforms mediating connections), as intermediary layers may absorb complexity that would otherwise require exclusionary network decisions. leverage.hypothesis: If outcomes from direct university-corporate partnerships () were made observable to intermediary organizations (, , ), it might reduce the system's ability to absorb complexity through intermediation without exposing comparative performance.

P1.summary: Tel Aviv AI & Deep Tech exhibits 6 observable stalls with Mediating instead of coupling and Re-proving instead of narrowing as primary behavioural patterns. 4 stabilisation stacks identified.

stacks.absorbed_signals: Uncertainty (regulatory compliance pathways), Pressure (legitimacy requirements in regulated environment), Complexity (multi-stakeholder coordination in financial services)

stacks.absorbed_signals: Uncertainty (regulatory evolution), Pressure (compliance requirements), Disruption (policy changes requiring adaptation)

P2.name: Detroit & Michigan Mobility & Auto Tech

stacks.description: Repeated capital deployment through partnerships and joint ventures (STALL_1, STALL_2) occurs alongside persistent intermediary layer (STALL_5). Partnership formation activity may sustain intermediary organizations by generating coordination demand; intermediary presence may enable continued partnership formation by reducing transaction costs. Both patterns absorb uncertainty about technology traj
stacks.absorbed_signals: Uncertainty (technology evolution), Complexity (multi-domain integration), Pressure (capital deployment expectations)

stacks.description: Dense intermediary layer (STALL_5) co-occurs with incumbent geographic and organizational continuity (STALL_6). Intermediary organizations may reduce pressure on incumbents to directly couple with new entrants by providing buffered engagement channels. Incumbent stability may sustain intermediary organizations by providing anchor clients and legitimacy. Configuration absorbs disruption signals whi
stacks.absorbed_signals: Disruption (new technology domains), Uncertainty (market structure evolution), Complexity (ecosystem fragmentation)

stacks.description: Investment announcements (STALL_7) co-occur with partnership formation (STALL_2) and capital deployment activity (STALL_1). Announcement activity may generate coordination demand that sustains partnership formation; partnership formation may enable continued announcement activity by providing declarative content. Combined pattern absorbs pressure to demonstrate strategic action without requiring o
stacks.absorbed_signals: Pressure (stakeholder expectations), Uncertainty (technology viability), Opportunity (signaling value)

stacks.description: Intermediary organizations (STALL_5) co-occur with programmatic infrastructure expansion (STALL_8). Incubator and association proliferation may sustain intermediary layer by generating service delivery activity; intermediary presence may enable continued infrastructure expansion by demonstrating ecosystem support demand. Combined pattern absorbs complexity and opportunity signals through activity
stacks.absorbed_signals: Complexity (diverse actor types), Opportunity (entrepreneurial activity), Pressure (ecosystem development expectations)

stacks.description: Partnership formation and coordination activity (STALL_2) co-occur with regulatory legitimacy-seeking (STALL_9). Waiting for regulatory clarity may sustain coordination activity by deferring unilateral action; coordination through partnerships may reduce pressure to act before regulatory enablement. Combined pattern absorbs uncertainty and risk while distributing decision authority.
stacks.absorbed_signals: Uncertainty (regulatory landscape), Pressure (deployment expectations), Complexity (legal risk)

leverage.hypothesis: If joint venture and partnership agreements were required to disclose decision authority, resource commitment timelines, and exit conditions, it might reduce the system's ability to absorb technology uncertainty through partnership formation without exposing governance trade-offs.

P2.summary: Detroit & Michigan Mobility & Auto Tech exhibits 7 observable stalls with Mediating instead of coupling and Stabilizing around incumbents as primary behavioural patterns. 5 stabilisation stacks identified.
